subject: [PATCH 3/3] nfsd: fix UAF in async copy cancel and shutdown

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's async copy cancel and shutdown functionality. The issue arises when an async copy is canceled or shut down, causing the reaper to move the copy off the async_copies list while another thread tries to access it, leading to a double free of the filecache object. To fix this, the patch modifies find_async_copy() to clear cp_clp and remove the copy from async_copies after incrementing its reference count, ensuring that the reaper can no longer observe the copy. Additionally, nfsd4_stop_copy() is modified to release the list-membership reference held by the reaper before calling cleanup_async_copy().

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: The reviewer, NeilBrown, expressed confusion about the patch's changes, specifically the extra refcount on the 'copy' structure and the barriers around assignment of ->cp_clp. He suggested that the comments should clarify what is being protected by these barriers.
reviews.raw_body:
This patch seems to make two changes.
One involves extra refcount on the "copy" structure, which is probably
sensible but hard for me to follow because the patch is so noisy.

The other is barriers around assignment of ->cp_clp which doesn't make
any sense to me at all, and the copious comments don't help with.
A barrier is always between two things.  Maybe we assign NULL to
something before testing something else, or maybe we test something
before an assignment.  The comments should make clear what the something
else is.  They don't (at least not to me).

I would rather two patches with less noise and more useful comments.

Thanks,
NeilBrown

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free (UAF) bug in the NFS daemon's async copy cancel and shutdown functionality. The issue arises when an async copy is canceled or shut down, causing the reaper to access a freed filecache object. To fix this, the patch modifies the find_async_copy function to clear the copy's reference count under the async lock and remove it from the async copies list before calling nfsd4_stop_copy. Additionally, the patch updates the cancel and shutdown functions to call nfs4_put_copy after nfsd4_stop_copy to release the list-membership reference held by the reaper.
